ail: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimele.
all: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Allar.
ally: verb. To unite or form a connection between (people or things), as between families by marriage, or between states by confederacy, league,
als: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tosk Albanian.
ays: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sorsogon Ayta.
ill: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranun.
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
las: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gur Lama.
lav: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Latvian.
lay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lama Bai.
lily: noun. Any of several flowers in the genus Lilium of the family Liliaceae, which includes a great many ornamental species.
luv: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Luwati.
sail: noun. A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along.
sal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Salishan languages.
sau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saleman.
saul: noun. Archaic form of sal (“the tree, Shorea Robusta”).
say: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saya.
sial: noun. The rocks rich in silicon and aluminum that form the upper layer of the earth's crust, which lies beneath all continental landmasses.
sill: noun. A breast wall; window breast; horizontal brink which forms the base of a window.
silly: adjective. Laughable or amusing through foolishness or a foolish appearance.
syli: noun. The currency of Guinea between 1971 and 1985, subdivided into 100 cauris.
ulva: noun. Any organism of the genus Ulva.
vas: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vasavi.
vau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vanuma.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vial: noun. A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vill: noun. The smallest administrative unit of land in feudal England, corresponding to the Anglo-Saxon tithing and the modern parish.
villa: noun. A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
villus: noun. A small projection from a membrane, particularly those found in the mucous membranes of the intestines.
vis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vishavan.
visa: noun. A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
visual: adjective. Related to or affecting the vision.
